description: A comprehensive direct response copywriting knowledge bank built from 9 elite email marketers. Pulls EVERY email with full body content, indexes by sender and copy type, and powers daily automated content production.

goals.primary: Pull ALL 5,716 emails from 9 senders with full body content into a persistent knowledge bank
goals.secondary: Power daily automated content engine: 1 Beehiiv newsletter, 1 ActiveCampaign autoresponder, 3 LinkedIn posts, 1 LinkedIn newsletter, 1 LinkedIn article per day
goals.expandable: User can add new senders anytime
goals.business_config: User needs to provide business details later for plug-and-play content generation

file_structure.ids_dir: ids/ - Contains {sender_key}.json files with all email IDs, subjects, dates for each sender
file_structure.content_dir: content/{sender_key}/ - Contains {email_id}.json files with full email content

scripts.build_database.py: Merges ID files with content files into consolidated swipe_database.json and swipe_compact.json. Run after saving new batches.
scripts.batch_reader.py: Tracks processing progress. Commands: status (progress bars), next [sender] [batch_size] (get next unprocessed IDs), remaining [sender]
scripts.save_emails.py: Saves raw Gmail API response to content files. Pipe JSON to stdin with sender_key as arg. Has clean_body() function.

outputs.swipe_database.json: Full consolidated database with all emails (bodies included for processed ones)
outputs.swipe_compact.json: Compact version with just subjects per sender (no bodies)
outputs.style_analysis.json: Sender DNA analysis with copywriting metrics

alex_hormozi.specialty: Offers, scaling, business acquisition, value equations

liam_ottley.specialty: AI agencies, automation, scaling AI businesses
liam_ottley.cleaning_notes: Remove URLs >100 chars, remove everything after 'Unsubscribe', remove '1 Albert Street, Auckland, CBD 1010' footer, remove 'Update your profile' links, remove invisible/zero-width chars, normalize to max 2 newlines

brian_kurtz.specialty: Direct response, classic copywriting, lifetime value

todd_brown.specialty: Marketing funnels, unique mechanisms, E5 method

lead_gen_jay.specialty: Cold email, lead generation, outbound, B2B

jon_buchan.specialty: Creative cold email, humor, pattern interrupts, charm

processing_workflow.step_1: Use batch_reader.py to get next batch of unprocessed IDs: python3 swipe-file/batch_reader.py next {sender_key} 5
processing_workflow.step_2: Read each email from Gmail using gmail_read_message with the message ID
processing_workflow.step_3: Save the email content using save_emails.py: echo '{gmail_json}' | python3 swipe-file/save_emails.py {sender_key}
processing_workflow.step_3_alt: Or save directly via Python by creating {email_id}.json in swipe-file/content/{sender_key}/ with fields: id, subject, date, sender, body (cleaned)
processing_workflow.step_6: Copy updated database to outputs: cp swipe-file/swipe_database.json swipe-file/swipe_compact.json /sessions/laughing-quirky-carson/mnt/outputs/
processing_workflow.batch_size: 5 emails at a time to avoid Gmail API rate limits
processing_workflow.rate_limiting: Read emails 1 at a time (not parallel) to avoid 500 errors. Add small delays if needed.
processing_workflow.cleaning: Use save_emails.py clean_body() or apply per-sender cleaning (strip long URLs, unsubscribe footers, invisible chars, normalize newlines)

email_content_schema.subject: Email subject line
email_content_schema.date: RFC 2822 date string from Gmail headers
email_content_schema.body: Cleaned email body text (URLs shortened, footers removed, whitespace normalized)
